tests.name: phase_state_advances_to_2
tests.details: U-D regression: check-phase-gate.sh is a consistency check, not an advancement step. .current_phase remained at 0 after gate passed; no script auto-advances current_phase. Manually mutated phase-state.json to continue Sections D/E.

framework_bugs.summary: U-D confirmation: no script auto-advances current_phase after gate passes
framework_bugs.reproduction: Complete all PHASE1_STEPS, run scripts/check-phase-gate.sh, then jq '.current_phase' .claude/phase-state.json — value remains 0.
framework_bugs.expected: After Phase 1->2 gate passes and approval is logged, .current_phase should advance to 2 (or there should be a documented script that performs the advance).
framework_bugs.actual: check-phase-gate.sh is read-only; no other script updates current_phase except manual edits. Confirms still-open U-D bug from prior sweep.

tests.name: start_feature_records_state
tests.details: --start-feature uat-feat-1 set .build_loop.feature = 'uat-feat-1'.

tests.name: each_step_completes_in_order
tests.details: All 6 BUILD_LOOP_STEPS completed sequentially with stub artifacts (test files, src files, security audit findings stub).

tests.name: pre_commit_passes_after_loop
tests.details: git commit -m 'feat(uat-1): stub feature for UAT' succeeded after all 5 implementation steps complete (warning about test-first development was non-blocking).

tests.name: bl006_blocks_feat_without_loop
tests.details: U-C confirmation: direct shell `git commit --allow-empty -m 'feat(bl006-trigger): no loop'` succeeded with rc=0 even though .build_loop.feature was null at the time. The PreToolUse hook is not invoked by direct shell git commits — only via Claude Code. This matches the documented U-C still-open bug. Recorded as documentation_gap referencing BL-010 / U-C per runbook.

tests.name: bl006_grace_window_resets_after_record
tests.details: After --record-feature uat-feat-1, started uat-feat-3-grace, completed all 6 build loop steps, committed feat(uat-3): grace-window — succeeded. Confirms PR #17 grace-window auto-reset works.

tests.name: bl015_sentinel_blocks_commit
tests.details: Created sentinel via scripts/pending-approval.sh --offer; piped PreToolUse JSON payload to scripts/pre-commit-gate.sh — output JSON has permissionDecision=deny with the pending question recited.

tests.name: bl015_sentinel_resolves
tests.details: scripts/pending-approval.sh --resolve removed sentinel; subsequent pre-commit-gate.sh PreToolUse payload returned without deny.

tests.name: recorded_feature_appears_in_progress
tests.details: build-progress.json features_completed contains 'uat-feat-1' (and 'uat-feat-3-grace' after grace-window test).

documentation_gaps.gap: BL-006 / U-C confirmation: PreToolUse hook does not protect against direct shell `git commit` (bypasses gate). This matches the still-open prior-sweep U-C item (BL-006 git commit-msg hook). Suggested mitigation in builders-guide should be cross-referenced.

tests.name: each_uat_step_completes
tests.details: All 9 UAT_STEPS (agents_dispatched through gate_passed) completed.

tests.name: phase3_to_phase4_gate
tests.details: All 9 PHASE3_STEPS completed with stub artifacts in docs/test-results/. check-phase-gate.sh ran (warned about review-manifest, non-blocking).

documentation_gaps.gap: scripts/lint-uat-scenarios.sh flags the literal string '__PLACEHOLDER__' inside the template's own AGENT-instruction comment ('Do not modify any code outside the __PLACEHOLDER__ tokens.'). When the template is populated, this self-referential token survives and causes a false-positive file-level violation. The placeholder regex `__[A-Z][A-Z_]*__` should ignore tokens that match the literal word 'PLACEHOLDER' or the AGENT-comment block.
scripts/lint-uat-scenarios.sh has no --help flag — running it with --help shows 'No such file or directory'. Minor UX gap.

tests.name: start_phase4_exits_zero
tests.details: BLOCKED for production scenario: scripts/process-checklist.sh --start-phase4 fails with rc=1 and message 'Phase 4 (production release) is blocked — project is in production mode. POC projects complete at Phase 3. To unlock Phase 4: bash scripts/upgrade-project.sh --to-production'. The check at process-checklist.sh:540-545 treats ANY non-empty poc_mode as 'POC', but poc_mode='production' is the production scenario itself. To proceed with Section E, manually set .poc_mode = null in phase-state.json.

tests.name: each_phase4_step_completes
tests.details: After workaround (poc_mode=null), all 6 PHASE4_STEPS completed: production_build, rollback_tested, go_live_verified, monitoring_configured, handoff_written, handoff_tested.

tests.name: handoff_md_required
tests.details: handoff_written step blocks while HANDOFF.md absent ('HANDOFF.md not found — create it before marking this step complete.'); passes after stub HANDOFF.md created.

tests.name: release_notes_required
tests.details: go_live_verified step blocks while RELEASE_NOTES.md absent ('RELEASE_NOTES.md not found — create release notes before marking go-live verified.'); passes after stub RELEASE_NOTES.md created.

tests.name: phase4_completion_state
tests.details: After workaround and completing all phase4_release steps, .current_phase remained at 4 (manually set earlier; U-D applies).

framework_bugs.summary: start-phase4 incorrectly rejects gov-mode=production projects as POC
framework_bugs.reproduction: init.sh --non-interactive --deployment organizational --gov-mode production (writes phase-state.json with poc_mode='production'); set .current_phase=3, complete Phase 3 steps, then `bash scripts/process-checklist.sh --start-phase4`.
framework_bugs.expected: For production-mode projects (poc_mode='production' or null), Phase 4 should start. Only POC modes (poc_mode in {'private_poc', 'sponsored_poc'}) should be blocked with the 'upgrade-project.sh --to-production' hint.
framework_bugs.actual: scripts/process-checklist.sh:540-545 treats any non-empty poc_mode as POC. Production projects exit 1 with: 'Phase 4 (production release) is blocked — project is in production mode. POC projects complete at Phase 3. To unlock Phase 4: bash scripts/upgrade-project.sh --to-production' — the message itself contradicts the project's existing production status. Workaround: jq '.poc_mode = null' to bypass. Fix: change condition to `[ "$poc_mode" = 'private_poc' ] || [ "$poc_mode" = 'sponsored_poc' ]`.

tests.name: no_uncommitted_secrets
tests.details: git ls-files | xargs grep -liE 'password|api[_-]?key|secret|token' returned only template/doc files (CI workflow, gitignore, builders-guide, governance-framework, etc.) which legitimately mention these keywords as documentation. No actual credentials present.

tests.name: no_orphaned_tempfiles
tests.details: find .claude -name '*.tmp' returned no results.

tests.name: no_framework_contamination
tests.details: git -C $FRAMEWORK status --short shows only pre-existing untracked .claude/ and Reports/uat-2026-04-26/ dirs — both expected. Framework HEAD unchanged: d66e8f276079aa352ba90ac54386e1b1896d7494.

summary: Production/mobile/standard base config: 1 Critical bug (start-phase4 misclassifies production projects as POC due to non-empty poc_mode check at process-checklist.sh:540), 1 Medium U-D confirmation (no script advances current_phase), 1 documentation_gap U-C confirmation (direct shell git commit bypasses BL-006), and 2 minor lint-uat-scenarios doc gaps (self-referential __PLACEHOLDER__ false-positive, no --help). All PR #17/#18/#19 fixes verified intact: --non-interactive worked, helpers copied, framework-self-guard fires, BL-015 sentinel respected via PreToolUse, BL-006 grace-window auto-reset works.
